trigger: Wave-120 Edgelord surfaced bug class 2 (pre-convention path-keys). Bolas/Trunks audit just surfaced bug class 1 (inline-quoted YAML lists). Parent requested a full sweep of cards/_characters/, cards/_symbols/, cards/_hubs/, cards/_artists/ for analogous broken-edge bugs.
bug_classes_checked: 1b: inline YAML lists with content (block-form preference)
3: broken wikilink targets in layer-node bodies

bug_1_inline_quoted_lists.rationale: Mechanical pattern, identical fix across all hits. Wave-92 codified block-form preference. Empty arrays `field: []` preserved inline per Edgelord spec.

bug_3b_dan_murayama_scott_corruption.rationale: Frontmatter line 22 had `<appears_on entry>related_hubs: []` (missing newline ate the field separator). Body wikilink on line 91 mirrored the broken stem. Fixed both sites to: (a) split into two frontmatter lines, (b) restore canonical [[134-raging-redcap]] wikilink.

bug_4a_son_goku_forward_gaps.rationale: son-goku character node lists 5 cards in appears_on; 2 had no `characters:` frontmatter field. Added `characters:\n  - son-goku` to both. Note: these cards are NOT vision-passed-tagged yet, but the character pointer is independent of vision-pass status and is reverse-resolved via the node's appears_on anyway, so adding the pointer hardens the bidirectional link now rather than waiting for re-enrichment.

bug_3_cross_vault_memory_wikilinks.verdict_not_a_bug: These are intentional citations to Alex's global memory wiki at ~/.claude/wiki/ and ~/.claude/projects/.../memory/. They do not resolve inside the BBL Obsidian vault but they are also not load-bearing edges — they are receipts/citations. Policy question for Alex: do we want a special citation syntax that doesn't show up as a broken wikilink in Obsidian's graph view? Out of scope for this pass.
bug_3_cross_vault_memory_wikilinks.recommendation: Consider a citation-syntax convention like `{memory:bbl-narrative-first-lairs}` (nolink-style) instead of `[[bbl-narrative-first-lairs]]` for the memory-store references. Would also benefit wiki-side rendering.

items.verdict: potential character node — Etrata is a canonical Ravnican vampire-assassin
potential character node — Iroas is mogis's twin god in Theros pantheon
potential character/faction node — Gatewatch is the canonical planeswalker collective
potential hub/concept node — foundational Ravnican law
potential hub node OR cross-vault wiki citation
potential bundle-anchor node or zodiac-subgroup hub
cross-project citation (the storefront), not a card-vault node
broken card wikilink — the stem should likely be `bt1-081-brolys-ring` if that card is in corpus; needs verification

bug_3_real_missing_node_candidates.recommendation: Each is a candidate Mr. Nodeley wave. Triage by load-bearing-ness; the Theros twin-gods iroas / mogis pair is the strongest near-term call (would let Mogis node's See-Also stop dangling). Stage for separate waves.

verdict: Mechanical-tier fixes shipped (bug 1 + bug 3b + bug 4a). Cross-vault memory wikilinks documented as policy-question-not-bug. Real missing-node wikilinks triaged into a future-Nodeley queue.
potion_rule_check: Each fix is the kind 'mechanical normalization the schema already specifies' — wave-92 block-form preference, the canonical appears_on stem format, the bidirectional pointer convention. No analytic overreach. The Nodeley-candidate triage was held BACK from this pass precisely because each would be a separate decision requiring its own canonical-source check.
